claim: Alpine glaciers are not melting significantly
accurate_info: Alpine glaciers have lost approximately 30% area since 1980 and continue experiencing accelerating mass loss (averaging -1-3 Gt/year)

claim: Snow cover in the Alps is stable or increasing
accurate_info: Alpine snow cover duration is declining approximately 2.3 days per year on average, with higher rates in southern ranges (3+ days/year)

claim: Alpine warming is due to natural cycles, not greenhouse gases
accurate_info: Alpine warming (+2.1°C since 1880, ~2x global average) is driven primarily by anthropogenic greenhouse gas forcing, with secondary natural oscillations

claim: Permafrost thaw in the Alps is reversible and not a concern
accurate_info: Alpine permafrost degradation is accelerating; thawing at 0.2°C/decade even at depth and triggers rockfall/infrastructure destabilization

claim: Alpine vegetation is spreading and shows ecosystem improvement
accurate_info: While treeline advances indicate some colonization, alpine plant communities are experiencing species loss, range compression, and altered phenology

claim: Avalanche hazards in the Alps are decreasing due to less snow
accurate_info: Avalanche hazard patterns are changing; reduced but more extreme precipitation events combined with rapid warming increase instability

claim: Alpine water supplies are secure despite glacier loss
accurate_info: 14 million downstream Alpine inhabitants depend on glacier meltwater; continued loss threatens summer water availability for agriculture, irrigation, and hydropower

claim: Local actions in mountain villages cannot affect global glacier trends
accurate_info: While driven primarily by global forcing, regional black carbon deposition, land use, and air pollution measurably accelerate local Alpine glacier loss
